message:
feat(perf): add conversation prefix cache to MessageRenderCache (PERF-2)

During streaming, only the tail content (current_response and
current_thinking) changes each frame while all finalized messages
remain identical. Previously build_conversation_content() re-iterated
the per-message cache for every finalized message even when nothing
changed, incurring allocation and lookup overhead proportional to
conversation length.

PERF-2 adds a prefix cache layer that stores the concatenated rendered
output of all finalized messages:

perf.rs:
- Add prefix, prefix_message_count, and prefix_generation fields to
  MessageRenderCache for caching the concatenated finalized output
- prefix_valid() checks staleness via message count and generation
  comparison — stale when messages added/removed, or after
  invalidate_all() (theme/resize/toggle changes)
- prefix_get() returns a clone of the cached prefix string
- prefix_set() stores new prefix content with current generation snapshot
- clear() and invalidate_all() both correctly reset/stale the prefix
- All new fields use interior mutability (RefCell/Cell) matching the
  existing per-message cache pattern

view.rs:
- Minor rustfmt reformatting of compute_key() call and memchr line

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
